main_cultures.name: Human Imperial Regency
religions: Deities of Law, Sun, and Civilization
main_cultures.architecture: Stone curtain walls, timber-framed houses, bustling marketplaces.

clan_or_surname.convention: Family name passed through the paternal line, often derived from a trade, place, or ancestor deed.

main_cultures.name: High Elven Reach
religions: Corellon Larethian, Deities of Magic and Arts
main_cultures.architecture: Vertical white marble spires integrated with ancient silver-leaf trees.

clan_or_surname.convention: House name, often a poetic compound in Elvish (Silverbough, Moonwhisper, Dawnweave). Some lineages use ancient Elvish house epithets.

main_cultures.name: Dwarven Clan-Hold
main_cultures.architecture: Massive geometric stone halls and deep-mining magma forges.

clan_or_surname.convention: Clan name passed through the paternal line, often stone or metal themed. Dwarves take deep pride in their clan above all else.

main_cultures.name: Halfling Shire-Folk
main_cultures.architecture: Earthen burrow-homes with round doors and thatched-roof cottages.

clan_or_surname.convention: Family name tied to home place or family trait, often whimsical and nature-themed. Passed through both lines.

main_cultures.name: Half-Elven Cosmopolitans
main_cultures.architecture: Refined urban townhouses, blended styles of human stone and elven wood-work.

clan_or_surname.convention: Often takes either the human family name or a simplified elven house name; blended names are also common in cosmopolitan cities.

main_cultures.name: Half-Orc Frontier-Kin
main_cultures.architecture: Reinforced log longhouses, stone watchtowers, and rugged frontier forts.

clan_or_surname.convention: Often bears either the orc clan name or a human family name; in frontier communities, a deed-name (earned title) is most respected.

main_cultures.name: Orcish War-Horde
main_cultures.architecture: Spiked timber palisades, bone-adorned totems, and hide tents.

clan_or_surname.convention: Clan names tied to Gruumsh's edicts or battle deeds; typically one word of Orcish, often referring to slaughter, conquest, or the eye.

subcultures.name: Drow Matriarchy
subcultures.architecture: Stalactite palaces suspended by magical chains over chasms; webs of obsidian and spider-silk span entire cavern ceilings.

clan_or_surname.convention: Matriarchal House name; males take their mother's house name. Social standing is entirely determined by the power of one's House. Format: [Given name] [House name].

subcultures.name: Wood Elf Enclave
subcultures.architecture: Tree-top platforms and vine-woven dwellings invisible from the forest floor; camouflaged by living bark and moss.

clan_or_surname.convention: Nature-based epithets rather than formal surnames; identified by their home grove or great tree. Format: [Given name] of the [place/tree/event].

subcultures.name: Duergar Clan-Hold
subcultures.architecture: Brutalist stone fortresses carved directly into cavern walls, lit by forge-fire and bioluminescent moss-lamps; no ornamentation.

clan_or_surname.convention: Stone-name clan, often describing a material or a grim deed. Passed through the paternal line, regarded with grim pride.

subcultures.name: Svirfneblin Deep-Warrens
religions: Callarduran Smoothhands (patron of miners and deep gnomes)
subcultures.architecture: Tightly fortified warrens hidden behind illusion-veiled entrances, built around gem-cutting workshops; passages are narrow and confusing by design.

clan_or_surname.convention: Gem-trade names tied to their craft; a personal nickname used among friends is common, while the full clan name is rarely spoken to outsiders.

subcultures.name: Dragonborn Dracocracy
subcultures.architecture: Obsidian monuments and grand dueling arenas; clan halls carved with draconic lineage glyphs.

clan_or_surname.convention: Draconic clan name; one long Draconic word, often the name of an ancestor wyrm or a great deed. Honor requires bearing the clan name proudly in all introductions.

subcultures.name: Aasimar Celestial-Order
subcultures.stance: Vigilant, Just, Watchful, Burdened by destiny
religions: Deities of Light, Justice, and the Upper Planes
subcultures.architecture: Sun-drenched cathedrals and golden-domed towers; light is sacred in their architecture.

clan_or_surname.convention: Virtue-based surnames or Light epithets; many aasimar bear names given by their celestial patron rather than family.

subcultures.name: Genasi Elemental-Sect
subcultures.architecture: Lava-proof halls (fire), underwater glass domes (water), cliff-side aeries (air), or deep stone vaults (earth); each subrace builds entirely differently.

clan_or_surname.convention: Elemental epithet rather than a family name; often a descriptor of their dominant element. Rarely use surnames — more commonly a title (Ignius of the Undying Flame).

subcultures.name: Goliath Mountain-Tribes
subcultures.architecture: Cyclopean stone huts and cliff-dwellings; camps move seasonally following game herds.

clan_or_surname.convention: Three-part goliath name: a birth-name (1–3 syllables), a name-gift from the tribe (an epithet describing a deed), and a clan name. Daily name used by friends is the nickname.

subcultures.name: Tabaxi Nomad-Caravans
religions: The Cat Lord (divine trickster and patron)
subcultures.architecture: Mobile wagons and yurt-tents; semi-permanent jungle lodges built in tree canopies when resting.

clan_or_surname.convention: Named after celestial phenomena or natural events; clan name is a place or major event (two or three words). Personal name is always poetic and descriptive.

subcultures.name: Firbolg Forest-Guardians
subcultures.architecture: Natural caves masked by illusions and living wood; settlements are invisible to those who don't know the signs.

clan_or_surname.convention: No surnames; identified by their grove, sacred tree, or protectorate. Format: [Name] of the [place]. True names are never spoken to outsiders.

subcultures.name: Kenku Rookery-Ghetto
subcultures.architecture: Interconnected rooftop lofts and high-density wooden shanties; warrens built into eaves and abandoned towers.

clan_or_surname.convention: No clan names; each kenku's name is a combination of mimicked sounds or an event they witnessed. Names function as unique identifiers within a rookery.

subcultures.name: Triton Undersea-Protectorate
subcultures.stance: Noble, Vigilant, Guarded against abyssal threats
subcultures.architecture: Coral fortresses and bioluminescent domes anchored to underwater peaks; spires of living coral and harvested deep-stone.

clan_or_surname.convention: Sea-name clan, often oceanic features or guardian duties. The protectorate title supersedes family name in formal address.

subcultures.name: Warforged Steel-Foundry
religions: The Becoming God (Church of the Becoming)
subcultures.architecture: Industrial barracks and geometric iron workshops; warforged settlements are functional above all else, no decorative features.

clan_or_surname.convention: Single word or designation; some adopt unit numbers or military ranks from the Last War. No clan names, but some warforged adopt maker-marks or house sigils.

regions: Old battlefields of the Last War

subcultures.name: Shifter Primal-Sect
subcultures.architecture: Fortified wooden lodges and sod-covered longhouses; pack-holds built in forest clearings with animal-totems at each entrance.

clan_or_surname.convention: Pack name tied to a totem animal or territory. Pack name is spoken after given name, e.g. Fang of the Wolf-Pack.

subcultures.name: Tiefling Outcast-Union
subcultures.architecture: Repurposed ruins and hidden urban districts; tiefling communities favor defensible buildings with multiple exits.

clan_or_surname.convention: Infernal surnames or virtue-names used as surnames; many tieflings adopt or invent surnames as a form of self-definition against their heritage.

regions: City subcultural quarters across the world

subcultures.name: Gnomish Tinkerer-Guilds
subcultures.architecture: Mechanical underground workshops filled with clockwork and pipes; surface workshops are chaotically organized but perfectly functional.

clan_or_surname.convention: Long complicated family surname (often a multi-syllable chain) plus a short personal nickname. The full name is only used in formal gnomish contexts.

regions: Gnomish Warrens of the Iron Hills

subcultures.name: Lizardfolk Tribal-Kin
subcultures.architecture: Mud-brick mounds and woven reed huts on raised platforms over water; no decoration, pure utility.

clan_or_surname.convention: No surnames; identified by their swamp or marsh territory. May add an epithetic descriptor tied to a hunt or kill. Tribe names are place-descriptive.

subcultures.name: Githyanki Astral-Legion
subcultures.architecture: Fortresses carved from the calcified husks of dead gods; red-and-silver aesthetic, martial in all things.

clan_or_surname.convention: Military rank precedes given name in formal address; gish-title for those blending magic and combat. Clan affiliations are military units, not family.

subcultures.name: Goblinoid War-Host
subcultures.alignment_dominance: Lawful Evil (Hobgoblin) / Neutral Evil (Goblin) / Chaotic Evil (Bugbear)
subcultures.stance: Efficient, Conquering, Strategic (Hobgoblin); Scavenging, Opportunistic (Goblin); Ambush-predator (Bugbear)
religions: Maglubiyet (The Mighty One, goblin god of war)
subcultures.architecture: Brutalist stone blockades and iron-reinforced bunkers (Hobgoblin); crude wooden palisades and cave dens (Goblin); hollow great-trees and cliff ambush lairs (Bugbear).

clan_or_surname.convention: Legion number and banner name; named for a conquest or a warlord. Hobgoblins use military designations, goblins use chaotic gang names.
